author: bgarciamoura
description: Turn any folder into a database — table, kanban board, gallery, calendar, timeline and list views powered by frontmatter. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: bgarciamoura
manifest.description: Turn any folder into a database — table, kanban board, gallery, calendar, timeline and list views powered by frontmatter.
manifest.name: Notion Bases

name: Notion Bases

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Formulas

- 13 date and time functions added to the formula engine (#40): `NOW()`, `TODAY()`, `DATE(year, month, day)`, `YEAR()`, `MONTH()`, `DAY()`, `HOUR()`, `MINUTE()`, `WEEKDAY()`, `DATEDIF()`/`DATEDIFF()`, `DATEADD()`, and `FORMATDATE()`
- Comparison operators (`<`, `>`, `<=`, `>=`, `=`) are now date-aware — comparing two date values compares their timestamps, not their string representations
- Date parsing now uses local time instead of UTC, eliminating the off-by-one-day error that appeared in timezones behind UTC
- Day difference calculations (`DATEDIF`) are DST-safe: the result reflects calendar days rather than elapsed hours

## Table View

- New per-column "Clip text" toggle: when enabled, cell text is truncated at the column boundary with an ellipsis instead of wrapping (#45)
- Clipped cells maintain a fixed row height even when the view-wide "Wrap text" option is on, keeping the table compact
- The entire cell area remains clickable for editing regardless of clip state
- Hovering a clipped cell shows the full text in a native browser tooltip — no need to open the cell to read long values

## Board View

- "Hide empty" and "Hide no-value" column toggles now persist in the view configuration (#43) — the board no longer resets to showing all columns on reload

## Templates

- A new "Template folder" setting per database restricts the template picker to a specific folder, so only relevant templates appear when creating rows (#42)
- When no folder is configured the picker continues to search the full vault as before

## Dates and Numbers

- Per-column "Format date" panel: pick from a set of presets or enter a custom moment.js pattern, with a live preview showing how the format renders on a real date (#46)
- Locale-aware defaults: date and number display now follows the Obsidian language setting rather than always using Brazilian formatting
- Four hardcoded Portuguese locale sites removed from the codebase — date and number formatting is now fully locale-driven
- Default date display no longer includes seconds, reducing visual noise in date columns

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/bgarciamoura/obsidian-notion-bases-plugin/compare/1.8.6...1.9.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## CSS Cleanup (Community Review)
- All 82 `!important` declarations removed — each moved to a dedicated rule whose selector carries tripled class specificity, which outranks core element/class rules without relying on importance; relative overrides (base vs hover) are preserved
- Both `:has()` selectors removed: the active row in select, multi-select and status dropdowns now gets an explicit `--active` modifier class at render time
- Duplicate declarations removed keeping the effective values

## Bug Fixes
- Hardcoded Portuguese "Limpar" in the status cell clear button replaced with the `select_clear` locale key

## Technical
- Verified in Obsidian via devtools: table view, sort panel, column menu and dropdown active-row highlight all render identically before/after

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/bgarciamoura/obsidian-notion-bases-plugin/compare/1.8.4...1.8.5

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Community Review Compliance (Round 2)
- Timer functions (`setTimeout`, `clearTimeout`, `setInterval`, `clearInterval`) now called via `window.*` and `requestAnimationFrame` via `window.requestAnimationFrame`, per the review guidance for popout window compatibility
- Removed every type assertion flagged as unnecessary: frontmatter access narrows through the new `isRecord()` type guard and scalar stringification goes through `stringifyScalar()` — no more `[object Object]` fallbacks
- Deprecated `setDynamicTooltip` dropped from the settings slider (the value is shown inline)

## Technical
- New `src/value-utils.ts` (`isRecord`, `stringifyScalar`)
- `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` updated to 0.4.1 and `typescript-eslint` to 8.63 so local lint reproduces the dashboard review bot; `no-unnecessary-type-assertion` enabled permanently
- Sentence-case false positives (digit-led counts, filename suffixes, dataview syntax literals) handled via the rule ignoreRegex option

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/bgarciamoura/obsidian-notion-bases-plugin/compare/1.8.3...1.8.4

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes
- **Wrap flicker is gone.** With "Wrap text" enabled, scrolling made the table flicker and eventually vanish: the fixed-row-height virtualizer predicted geometry that wrapped (taller) rows kept invalidating, so every frame picked a different slice. When any visible column has wrap on, the table now bypasses the virtualizer and renders all rows outright (#21)
- **Folder arrangement respects the active filter.** "Preview & apply to existing rows" now operates on the filtered row set instead of every row in the database (#22)

## Note
This fix was written in May but the pull request (#29) was never merged — it shipped now, brought up to date with everything in between.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/bgarciamoura/obsidian-notion-bases-plugin/compare/1.8.2...1.8.3

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Embeds: Every View Type Available Per Block
- The `type:` parameter of `nb-database` code blocks now accepts `board`, `gallery`, `calendar`, `timeline` and `chart` in addition to `table` and `list` — previously anything else was silently ignored and fell back to the default view
- Forced-type embeds inherit their configuration from the database view whose type matches the request (board `groupBy`, calendar date column, …) instead of always cloning the first view
- One note can now embed the same database as a board grouped by status while another embeds it as a calendar by due date, without duplicating `_database.md`
- Closes #33

## Technical
- Block parsing extracted into the pure `parseEmbedBlock` function with 12 new unit tests
- `getLanguage` added to the Obsidian test mock

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/bgarciamoura/obsidian-notion-bases-plugin/compare/1.8.1...1.8.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Community Review Compliance (Round 1)
- `minAppVersion` raised from 1.4.0 to 1.8.7 — the code already relied on newer APIs (`getLanguage`, `Workspace.revealLeaf`, `FileManager.trashFile`, `Vault.getFileByPath`/`getFolderByPath`, `FileManager.processFrontMatter`) that the automated review checks against the manifest
- Popout window compatibility sweep across 24 files: DOM access goes through `activeDocument` and the `createEl`/`createDiv`/`createSpan` helpers
- Release workflow now attests build provenance for `main.js`, `manifest.json` and `styles.css`; `demo-vault.zip` is no longer shipped as a release asset
- Closes #34

## Bug Fixes
- Subfolder toggle now works on single-view databases — the single-table shortcut dropped `includeSubfolders` from the row-loading hook dependencies (#28)
- Full i18n coverage for formula descriptions and tooltips — `FORMULA_REF` descriptions, drag/search/conjunction tooltips and the folder picker placeholder were hardcoded strings (#30)
- Locale strings rephrased to satisfy the sentence-case rule with default options

## Technical
- Obsidian runtime globals declared in the ESLint config
- Timer refs typed as `number` instead of `ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>`

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/bgarciamoura/obsidian-notion-bases-plugin/compare/1.8.0...1.8.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Folder Context Menu
- Right-click any folder in the file explorer → new "Create database here" item creates a Notion Bases database in that folder
- Item is hidden automatically when the folder already hosts a database
- New `ctx_create_database` locale key across all 7 languages
- Closes #19

## Live Template Placeholders
- `{{columnId}}` tokens in a note body resolve to the cell's current frontmatter value in reading view
- Editing a cell in the table updates every placeholder immediately — the file on disk is never rewritten
- Built-ins `{{title}}`, `{{folder}}`, `{{date[:fmt]}}`, `{{time[:fmt]}}` now render live too
- Type-aware formatting: numbers with NumberFormat, dates with moment, multiselect joined, checkbox as ✓/✗
- `select` and `status` columns render as colored pills matching the table view
- Optional format suffix for dates/numbers: `{{due:DD/MM/YYYY}}`
- Skips code, pre, script, style, kbd and `nb-database` embeds
- Source/edit mode still shows the raw `{{token}}` — intentional tradeoff that keeps body edits safe
- Closes #18

## Auto-Arrange Files into Subfolders (DBFolder-Style)
- New "Folder arrangement" section in the database settings modal
- Configure an ordered list of frontmatter properties (text, select, status, date) that become a nested folder hierarchy
- On property change, the row file is moved to match: `status=Done, priority=High` → `<db>/Done/High/row.md`
- Empty values stop the hierarchy at the last filled level; clearing a value walks the file back up
- Off by default — silently moving files would be surprising
- "Preview & apply to existing rows" modal lists every planned `old → new` path before any batch move
- `_database.md` is excluded from arrangement
- Folders are created on demand; target-path collisions are skipped
- Moves go through `fileManager.renameFile` so Obsidian's link resolver stays consistent
- In-progress path set guards against rename → metadata → rename loops
- Closes #22

## Bug Fixes
- DatabaseRoot no longer clobbers its own state on self-writes — config edits (view reorder, settings, etc.) are now persisted reliably without being overwritten by the stale metadata-cache echo

## Technical
- New `src/live-placeholders.ts` with a `MarkdownRenderChild` that subscribes to `metadataCache.on('changed')` and re-resolves spans in place (post-processors do NOT auto-rerun on metadata change — this is the load-bearing piece)
- New `src/format-cell-value.ts` with `formatCellValueText` and `formatCellValueNodes` — shared type-aware formatter used by the post-processor; pills emitted as DOM nodes for select/status/multiselect
- New `src/folder-arrangement-preview-modal.ts` — batch preview UI
- New `computeArrangedPath`, `applyArrangement`, `applyArrangementToAll`, `previewArrangement`, `findGoverningDatabase` helpers in `DatabaseManager`
- `DatabaseConfig` extended with optional `folderArrangement: { enabled, propertyIds }`
- Global `metadataCache.on('changed')` listener in `main.ts` routes row changes to `applyArrangement`
- File-menu event hooked in `main.ts` for the folder context menu
- `MarkdownPostProcessor` registered in `main.ts` for the live placeholders
- `sanitizeSegment` utility strips invalid filename characters from folder segments
- New unit tests: `tests/folder-arrangement.test.ts`, `tests/format-cell-value.test.ts` (15 cases total)
- Internationalized across all 7 locales (en, pt-BR, es, fr, de, zh, ja)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/bgarciamoura/obsidian-notion-bases-plugin/compare/1.7.0...1.8.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Per-Database Row Templates
- New gear button in the view tabs bar opens a Database settings modal
- Pin a default template file to apply when new rows are created
- Optional "Ask on create" toggle shows a fuzzy template picker per row (with a "No template" option)
- Template body is appended to the new note with `{{title}}`, `{{date}}`, `{{time}}`, `{{folder}}` substituted
- Moment formats supported for date/time (e.g. `{{date:YYYY-MM-DD}}`)
- Config persists as `templatePath` and `askTemplateOnCreate` in the database frontmatter
- CSV import keeps using bare createNote to avoid per-row prompts
- Template picker excludes other `_database.md` files
- Closes #15

## Conditional Formatting
- Define per-view rules that colorize cells and cards based on their values
- Table view: paints cell backgrounds and text when rules match
- Board, Gallery, and List views: paints a colored border-left on the card
- Rules panel reuses the same operators as the filter engine (contains, equals, greater than, is empty, etc.)
- Select options read with label fallback for correct matching

## Optional Pagination
- Switch between infinite scroll and paged mode per view
- Configurable rows per page (50 / 100 / 200)
- Toggle available in the view configuration

## Auto-Save Indicator
- Visual indicator in the toolbar shows when edits are being saved to frontmatter
- Clear feedback when writes land vs when they're in-flight

## Bug Fixes
- Views no longer reset when the schema is edited (#16)
- Conditional format panel now reads select options with label fallback

## Technical
- New `src/database-settings-modal.ts` and `src/template-picker-modal.ts` for the template flow
- `createNoteWithTemplate` helper centralizes template application on row creation
- Conditional formatting evaluator shared across Table, Board, Gallery, and List
- Pagination state persisted in ViewConfig
- `useDatabaseRows` syncs views from disk to prevent reset on schema edits
- Internationalized across all 7 locales (en, pt-BR, es, fr, de, zh, ja)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/bgarciamoura/obsidian-notion-bases-plugin/compare/1.6.0...1.7.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Dataview Inline Field Support (read + write)
- Reads `key:: value` inline fields from note bodies — no frontmatter required
- Three syntaxes supported: standalone (`Key:: Value`), bracketed (`[key:: value]`), parenthesized (`(key:: value)`)
- Edits write back to the original position in the note, preserving the original format
- Deleting a cell value removes the inline field from the note body
- Frontmatter always takes precedence on conflicts (same key in both)
- Falls back to frontmatter write if the original inline field can't be located
- Comma-separated inline values auto-convert to arrays for multiselect columns
- Fields inside code blocks (fenced and inline) are correctly ignored
- Schema inference detects inline fields when creating new databases
- Opt-in via Settings → "Read dataview inline fields" (off by default, zero cost when disabled)

## Quick Add Command
- New "Quick Add Row" command available from the Command Palette anywhere in Obsidian
- Fuzzy-search database picker → dynamic form based on the database schema
- Supports title + text, number, select, multiselect, status, date, checkbox, url, email, phone fields
- "Create" closes the modal; "Create and open" opens the new note in a tab
- Internationalized across all 7 locales

## Manual Row Reordering in Table View
- Drag handle (⠿) in the select column for reordering rows
- Custom order persisted in `rowOrder` within the ViewConfig
- Automatically disabled when sorts or hierarchy are active
- Long-press context menu added to List view as part of this work

## Performance Improvements
- Chunked data loading via `useDatabaseRows` hook (200 notes/batch, 50ms debounce on vault events)
- Debounced filters and search by 200ms across all 6 views + global filter in Table
- `React.memo` on heavy components: CellRenderer, BoardCard, GalleryCard, TimelineBar, ListRow
- Callbacks stabilized with `useCallback` to prevent unnecessary re-renders

## Bug Fixes
- List view mobile row borders now extend full scrollable width

## Technical
- New `src/inline-fields.ts` parser — pure functions, no Obsidian dependencies, fully testable
- `getNoteData()` refactored to async with sync fallback (`getNoteDataSync`) for lookup/rollup resolvers
- `updateNoteField()` refactored as router: inline fields → body write; others → frontmatter write
- `InlineFieldMeta` tracks format, rawKey, rawValue, lineNumber, fullMatch for precise write-back
- `QuickAddModal` extends Obsidian `Modal` with dynamic form generation from schema
- 34 new unit tests (inline field parser + type inference)
- Internationalized across all 7 locales (en, pt-BR, es, fr, de, zh, ja)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/bgarciamoura/obsidian-notion-bases-plugin/compare/1.5.1...1.6.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

### Relation columns now use file names instead of column values (#12)

When configuring a Relation column, the "Source field for values" dropdown allowed selecting any column from the target database. This was incorrect — Relations should always reference **records** (file names) from the target database, not values from an arbitrary column.

**What changed:**
- The "Source field for values" dropdown is no longer shown when configuring a Relation (it still appears for Lookup columns)
- Relations now always use the file name as the value source
- Existing Relations configured with a wrong source field are **auto-migrated** when the database loads — no manual reconfiguration needed

Thanks to [@bangbangbanggit](https://github.com/bangbangbanggit) for reporting this issue.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/bgarciamoura/obsidian-notion-bases-plugin/compare/1.5.0...1.5.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Charts View (7th view type)
- 3 chart types: **Bar**, **Line**, **Pie** — all pure SVG, zero external dependencies
- Configurable X axis (categories) and Y axis (values or count)
- 4 aggregation functions: sum, average, min, max
- Smart Y axis ticks (integer for counts, k/M suffix for large numbers)
- Tooltips with category name on hover (e.g. "Planejamento: 283,000")
- Responsive via ResizeObserver, full mobile support with BottomSheet config panel
- Sort order respected in chart when active; defaults to value descending

## Subtasks / Sub-rows (hierarchy)
- Mark any self-relation column as "hierarchical" to enable parent-child nesting
- **Table view:** expand/collapse toggles (▶/▼), indentation up to 3 levels, inline "+" button on hover
- **List view:** indentation with expand/collapse toggles
- "Add sub-row" action in right-click/long-press context menu (desktop + mobile)
- Sort-by-group: parents sorted among each other, children sorted within their parent
- Independent filtering: each row evaluated alone
- Circular reference detection prevents infinite loops
- Other views (Board, Gallery, Calendar, Timeline, Chart) treat rows flat

## Rollup Columns
- 7 aggregation functions: sum, count, avg, min, max, count_values, list
- Draggable floating configuration panel with 3-step setup
- Read-only computed values that resolve automatically across all 7 views

## List View Redesign
- Two-line layout: title on top, property badges wrapped below
- Cleaner visual separation between rows
- Props displayed as compact badges with label:value format

## Technical
- Pure SVG chart rendering (~400 LOC, no external charting library)
- `hierarchy-utils.ts` with tree building, circular ref detection, max depth enforcement
- `createNote()` now accepts optional `initialFrontmatter` for sub-row creation
- 15 new unit tests for hierarchy logic
- Internationalized across all 7 locales (en, pt-BR, es, fr, de, zh, ja)
releases.name: v1.5.0 — Charts View, Subtasks & Rollup Columns

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v1.4.0 — Audio, Video, Time Support & Mobile Polish

### New column types

- **Audio column** — inline player with folder-based file picker, supports 7 audio formats (mp3, wav, ogg, flac, m4a, aac, webm)
- **Video column** — play icon + filename link, tap opens modal player, folder-based file picker

### Calendar time support (Google Calendar-style)

- Date fields now accept `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm` format with a 24h time toggle in the cell editor
- Weekly view completely redesigned with vertical time slots (48px/hour), positioned timed cards, and an all-day row at the top
- Current time indicator line spans the full week view and updates every minute
- Auto-scroll smoothly to current time or earliest card when entering the week view
- Time badges displayed on cards in both month and week views
- Cards sorted by time within each day; drag-and-drop preserves the time component

### Mobile optimizations

- Board columns: scroll-snap between columns with auto-scroll during drag
- Long-press context menu (BottomSheet) on board cards and table rows with Open, Duplicate, Delete actions
- Calendar: reduced cell height, 1 card + "+N" badge, tap to expand, long-press day actions
- Timeline: sidebar hidden on mobile, bar tap opens BottomSheet with label and actions
- Week view adapts to mobile with compact gutter and smaller fonts

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's New

### Two-Way Relations
Relation columns now support **two-way sync**. When configuring a relation, enable the "Two-way relation" checkbox to automatically create a paired column in the target database. Editing values on either side keeps both in sync.

### Multi-Value Relations
Relation columns now store **multiple linked notes** per cell. The dropdown uses toggle selection with checkmarks, and cells display multiple badges. Fully backward compatible with existing single-value relations.

### Self-Relations
Databases can now relate to themselves, enabling use cases like parent-child hierarchies or linked items within the same database.

### Mobile Touch Drag-and-Drop (Board View)
Cards and columns in the Board view can now be **dragged on mobile** using touch. A ghost preview follows your finger with visual feedback for the drop target. Works on both Android and iOS via Obsidian mobile.

## Bug Fixes

- **Relation cell display** — Fixed `[object Object]` appearing in relation cells when values were non-string types
- **Timeline zoom buttons** — Restyled to match the calendar segmented control design
- **Relation picker** — Root-level databases no longer show as blank entries
- **ESLint compliance** — Resolved all errors flagged by the Obsidian community review bot (no-base-to-string, no-static-styles-assignment, unused variables)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new in 1.1.1

### Bug fixes
- **Root-level database blank page** — databases created at the vault root no longer show a blank page when a note contains internal `notion-bases-embeds` frontmatter
- **Schema corruption protection** — sanitize schema on read to filter out corrupted entries and internal fields; broaden inference exclusion to skip any `notion-bases*` prefixed keys
- **Board view not grouping by status** — status columns were excluded from option extraction during schema inference, resulting in empty kanban groups

### New features
- **Card limit per board column** — click the card count badge to set a maximum; counter turns red when reached, cards beyond the limit are hidden with a "+N more" button
- **Multi-value filter picker** — "Is" and "Is not" operators on select/multiselect/status columns now show a checkbox picker for selecting multiple values at once
- **"Contains" and "Does not contain" filter operators** for select, multiselect, and status columns
- **Color picker for all badge columns** — select, multiselect, and status cells now have a color palette with custom color input, inline option creation and deletion
- **Auto-contrast badge text** — text color automatically adjusts to white or dark based on background luminance

### Other improvements
- Remove `DEBUG_MOBILE` flag that was forcing mobile layout on desktop
- Fix sticky checkbox column transparency on horizontal scroll
- Flicker-free color editing using local state buffering
- Updated installation instructions (BRAT as primary method)
- i18n: added `color_custom`, `select_create_placeholder`, board limit labels across all 7 locales

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
1.0.8

  New

  - Demo vault — Download demo-vault.zip from the release assets to explore all plugin features. Includes 4 databases, 40+ notes, cover images,
  and pre-configured views.

  Bug Fixes

  - Fixed race condition where toggling view settings (like "Include subfolders") would briefly show correct data then revert — loadData no longer
   overwrites local view state with stale frontmatter data
  - Fixed subfolder detection failing with special folder names by using recursive folder.children traversal instead of path string matching
  - Fixed folder column displaying truncated names for folders with numeric prefixes (e.g. "01 - Projects")

  Code Quality

  - Added loadVersion abort mechanism to prevent stale async loads from applying results across all 6 view components
  - Removed unnecessary async from resolveLookupsForRows and cleaned up await calls in all components
  - Enabled @typescript-eslint/require-await rule and recommendedWithLocalesEn config from eslint-plugin-obsidianmd
  - Fixed sentence case for UI text (picker_root)
  - Updated comparison table in README to accurately reflect core Bases formula capabilities

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
1.0.7

  Bug Fixes

  - Fixed race condition where loadData overwrote local view state (e.g. includeSubfolders) with stale frontmatter data before writeConfig
  completed
  - Added load version abort mechanism to prevent stale async loads from applying results
  - Changed subfolder detection to use recursive folder.children traversal instead of path string matching, fixing issues with special folder
  names
  - Fixed folder column displaying truncated names for folders with numeric prefixes (e.g. "01 - Projects")

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
1.0.6

  New Features

  - Include Subfolders — New per-view toggle to recursively include notes from subfolders in any database. When active:
    - All notes from nested subfolders appear alongside root-level notes
    - A relative folder path indicator is shown below titles in all 6 views (Table, Board, Gallery, List, Calendar, Timeline)
    - A sortable Folder column is added to the table view, displayed before the Name column
  - i18n — Added support for Spanish, French, German, Chinese (Simplified) and Japanese

  Bug Fixes

  - Fixed subfolder detection when database is located at vault root
  - Fixed toggle not loading subfolder notes due to stale state in event handlers

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
1.0.5 — Fix language detection

  Bug fix

  - Fixed: plugin showing Portuguese UI for all users — The i18n system was using moment.locale() which could return unexpected values. Now uses Obsidian's official getLanguage() API to correctly detect the app language.
  - Moved all remaining hardcoded Portuguese strings (commands, picker, view name) to the i18n system. Users with Obsidian set to English will now see English commands and UI text.

  Affected areas

  - Command palette: "Create new database" / "Open database" now display in the correct language
  - Database picker modal placeholder
  - View display name fallback

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
● 1.0.4 — Review fixes & README overhaul

  What changed

  - README rewritten — Added side-by-side comparison table vs core Bases plugin, highlighting all 6 views, 15 column types, formulas, relations,
  and features the core plugin doesn't have
  - Funding simplified — Removed Ko-fi and GitHub Sponsors, keeping only Buy Me a Coffee
  - Release script — Added npm run release to automate version bump, type-check, commit, tag and push

  Lint & review fixes

  - Removed unnecessary await on synchronous readConfig() calls across all view components
  - Fixed remaining sentence case violations in i18n strings
  - Added release.mjs to eslint ignores (Node.js script, not plugin code)
  - Removed unused eslint-disable directive on onClose

  No functional changes

  This release addresses code quality feedback from the Obsidian community plugin review and improves the project's documentation for
  discoverability.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
1.0.3 — Community plugin review fixes

  What changed

  - Lint compliance: Added descriptions to all eslint-disable directive comments explaining why each suppression is necessary
  - Sentence case enforcement: Updated all i18n strings (EN + PT-BR) to follow Obsidian's sentence case convention for UI text
  - Type safety: Replaced any casts with proper types (MarkdownView, React.CSSProperties), removing disallowed @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
  suppressions
  - Async cleanup: Removed unnecessary async from functions that had no await expressions (readConfig, onClose, handleChangeColumnType, embed
  processor)
  - File deletion: Replaced Vault.delete() with FileManager.trashFile() to respect the user's file deletion preference
  - Dead code removal: Removed unused imports (SortingFn, NoteRow)

  No functional changes

  This release addresses code quality feedback from the Obsidian community plugin review. There are no changes to features or behavior.
